text.title: threads-growth Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for threads-growth as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Just cross-posting content from Twitter without adaptation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Twitter content is declarative and polished. Threads culture rewards casual, curious, rough-edged posts. Verbatim cross-posts feel out of place and underperform.

failure-mode.description: Being too promotional — product pitches in posts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Threads's algorithm heavily penalizes promotional content. The platform norms are 95% value, 5% promo. Any promotional feel reduces distribution.

failure-mode.description: Posting once a day instead of 5+ posts daily.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Threads requires higher post volume than LinkedIn or Twitter because the feed is highly active and content decays quickly. One daily post is not enough for the algorithm.

failure-mode.description: Using a formal or corporate tone.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Threads culture is conversational and casual. Corporate language signals inauthenticity and the audience does not engage.
